title: Inference, Annotations, Literals, and Widening · Mastery Check
summary: Model service categories, thresholds, identifiers, and configuration without widening away domain meaning. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a literal-safe service configuration model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.
Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.
Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A service config begins as {status: "degraded", retries: 4, regions: ["west"]}. Preserve the status literal union and readonly region intent while proving a changed "offline" record fails at the correct boundary.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use lowercase primitive types and distinguish them from boxed wrapper object types.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
